# Dr. Michael Kurr

> Founder of Dr. Kurr Advisory. Fractional C-level executive and transformation leader in Pharma & Life Sciences with 20+ years of experience across Boehringer Ingelheim, Novartis, and global pharma. Published author and expert in building world-class organizations.

## About

Dr. Michael A. Kurr is a Germany-based executive who founded Dr. Kurr Advisory in January 2026 — an independent platform for fractional C-level and advisory mandates in Pharma & Life Sciences. He currently serves as Fractional Chief Commercial Officer at Lemna Bio (computational drug design, Lausanne) and as Fellow at THENA Capital (UK-based MedTech investor).

Previously, Dr. Kurr was Global Head of Human Pharma Services at Boehringer Ingelheim (2019-2025), where he built the entire go-to-market services organization from scratch, scaling it tenfold within four years to full geographical coverage. External benchmarking studies recognized it as one of the leading content supply chains in the pharmaceutical industry.

His career includes senior roles at Novartis (Head of Business Service Excellence Region Europe, Senior Global Program Lead), and earlier work at Schuh & Co. management consultancy and the University of St. Gallen.

## Education

- Doctorate (Dr. oec. HSG) — University of St. Gallen, Economics & Technology Management (2000-2003)
- Executive MBA — University of St. Gallen (2005-2006)
- Dipl.-Ing. — RWTH Aachen University, Production Management (1992-1999)
- Executive Certificate in Digital Business — MIT Sloan School of Management (2024)
- Leading Digital Business Transformation, TransformTECH, Digital Strategy and Analytics — IMD (2019-2020)

## Core Expertise

- Corporate transformation & organizational design
- Process-oriented reorganization / business process reengineering
- Cooperation management & strategic alliances
- Global services delivery & content supply chains
- Digital factory & content operations
- Pharmaceutical go-to-market strategies
- AI and data science in pharma
- Change management & people development
- Fractional C-level leadership (CCO, CTO, COO)

## Advisory Services

Dr. Kurr Advisory offers six core service areas:

1. **Fractional C-Level Leadership** — Part-time CCO, CTO, or COO mandates with embedded executive presence (2-3 days/week)
2. **Global Operating Model Design** — Scalable models balancing centralization with local agility, including shared services and offshoring
3. **AI & Digital Strategy** — Practical AI adoption within pharma compliance frameworks, agentic AI, content supply chain automation
4. **Commercial & Medical Operations** — Go-to-market redesign, content factories, omnichannel engagement at scale
5. **Strategic Partnerships & Alliances** — Research-backed cooperation management, vendor consolidation, partnership governance
6. **Organizational Design & People Strategy** — Building global teams, talent strategy, change management, leadership development

## Leadership Philosophy

Five core traits: Transparency, Respect, Empowerment, Fairness, Vision.
Core equation: "Structure follows process follows strategy."
Three pillars: People, Process, Technology.

## Publications

### Books (Carl Hanser Verlag, co-authored with Prof. Schuh & Prof. Friedli)
- "Prozessorientierte Reorganisation" (2006) — ISBN 978-3-446-40720-6
- "Kooperationsmanagement" (2005) — ISBN 978-3-446-40036-8

### Academic Papers
- "Success factors for collaboration management in manufacturing companies" — IJSOM Vol. 2(3), 2006
- "Aufbau und Nutzen von Kooperationen: ApoKop" — Schuh & Co., 2007
- "Netzwerke in Virtuellen Fabriken" — SATW-Bulletin, 2004

### Dissertation
- "Potentialorientiertes Kooperationsmanagement in der Zulieferindustrie" — University of St. Gallen, 2003

## Key Achievements

- Built Boehringer Ingelheim's Global Human Pharma Services from zero to full global scale in 4 years
- Organization expanded 10x under his leadership
- Built "#1 content supply chain in the industry" per external benchmarks
- Transformed go-to-market operations across 5 major global organizations
- Published 2 books and 3+ academic papers on transformation and cooperation management

## The Transformation Circus

An essay by Dr. Michael Kurr on leading corporate transformation in the age of AI. Drawing on 15+ years of pharma transformation experience at Boehringer Ingelheim and Novartis, it explores the metaphor of a circus — where leaders juggle processes, build teams of "smart apes," tame chaos monkeys, and keep executive lion tamers engaged. Key thesis: AI scales execution, but your operating model decides what gets scaled.
